assertions.summary: init wrote an example config at the requested fixture path and a second run without --force refused to overwrite it with a clear error.
assertions.details: Used isolated fixture config /home/fsos/code-lean/ai-tools/ProxyPilot/.factory/validation/terminal-operator-console/user-testing/fixtures/cli-shell/20260405T121714Z/proxypilot-rs.toml. Successful init printed the written path. Re-running init returned exit code 1 with "config already exists ... (pass --force to overwrite)". The captured config file includes server.bind=127.0.0.1:8318, state.path=proxypilot-rs.state.toml, and codex.upstream_base_url=https://api.openai.com.

assertions.summary: Add, list, activate, and remove commands kept saved active-account state coherent, including promotion back to the remaining account and explicit empty-state output after removing the final account.
assertions.details: primary became active when first added; list output marked it with `*`. After activating backup, list output switched the marker to backup and the saved state snapshot shows active_account="backup". Removing backup reported "local active account is now `primary`" and the next state snapshot shows active_account="primary". Removing primary reported "no local active account remains" and follow-up list output showed "No local Rust accounts saved yet." with an empty accounts snapshot.

assertions.summary: Negative refresh paths behaved clearly for static and missing accounts, but the required refresh-success path could not be exercised within the isolated fixture because refresh-codex is hardwired to the live OpenAI OAuth refresh endpoint.
assertions.details: Imported a refreshable local fixture account and added a static account inside the assigned fixture state. Refreshing the static account failed cleanly with "account `static-local` is static and has no refresh token"; refreshing a missing account failed with "no saved Codex account named missing-local". Follow-up list/state evidence shows saved state remained unchanged after both failures. The assertion remains blocked because the CLI refresh path uses the built-in OpenAI OAuth refresh endpoint and the supported config surface does not expose a way to redirect refresh-codex to a local mock endpoint without live credentials.

blockers: VAL-CLI-003 success-path refresh is not isolatable in this shell fixture: refresh-codex uses the built-in live OpenAI OAuth refresh endpoint, and the allowed CLI/config surface provides no local mock override. Live device-login/private credentials were explicitly disallowed, so the refresh-success portion of the assertion was recorded as blocked with evidence.
overallSummary: Validated 3 CLI shell assertions for terminal-operator-console: VAL-CLI-001 pass, VAL-CLI-002 pass, VAL-CLI-003 blocked due to non-mockable live refresh dependency. Report and evidence were written under the requested flow-report and mission evidence paths.
